BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Quick back-of-napkin on whether the Occupational Therapy consulting side income covers its own costs before the year-end review. Pre-CPA review.

CurrentState.Note: Rough estimate only. Need to revisit with CPA once 2025 actuals are closed. PSLF status is the real constraint, not these numbers.

Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: Add one more consulting person at $1000/mo while staying at BRC
Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: Fixed costs $313 + $100 home-office bump = $413. At $1,500/person, $413 / $1,500 = 0.28 → 1 person minimum.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Impact: Minimal. Well within buffer with the existing retainer.

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: Worst project - leave BRC and consult full-time (PSLF risk)
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Numbers fine, but leaving BRC pauses PSLF at 7/10 years and forfeits ~$38K. Not worth it before 2028.

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: Break-even is well under one full person at current cost structure. PSLF is the binding constraint, not break-even. Stay hybrid until PSLF completion in 2028.
ActionItems: Complete HTCC exam December 2026 to increase consulting rate to $150/hr
Revisit after year-end with CPA for proper modeling
